conflict: Every household needs papers, witnesses, and internal consistency before a looming storm and cutoff date, but most lives on the char were never built for bureaucratic neatness.
consequence: Residents begin trading testimony, hoarding documents, and informally sorting themselves into those whose stories can be cleaned and those whose cannot.
must_land_beats: Aminul posts the next deadline and raises the practical stakes.
The chapter shows multiple households under paperwork stress rather than narrowing to a single confrontation.
Weather pressure makes paper feel physically vulnerable, not abstractly valuable.
Halima sees the island reorganizing itself around administrative survival.
objective: Buy time and keep the island from dissolving into panic as the documentation deadline approaches.
